title: Understand why integration tests on MacOS require '127.0.0.1' instead of 'localhost'
body:
<!-- If you need to report a security issue please visit https://docs.dapr.io/operations/support/support-security-issues -->

## In what area(s)?

<!-- Remove the '> ' to select -->

> /area runtime

> /area operator

> /area placement

> /area docs

/area test-and-release

## What version of Dapr?

<!-- Delete all but your choice -->

> 1.1.x
> 1.0.x
> edge: output of `git describe --dirty`

## Expected Behavior

Integration tests on MacOS can run without `DAPR_HOST_IP` override and point to `localhost` from test code.


## Actual Behavior

Integration tests are flakier if use `localhost`.


## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

Change the test code to point to `localhost` and remove the `DAPR_HOST_IP` override in GH workflow.

## Release Note

<!-- How should the fix for this issue be communicated in our release notes? It can be populated later. -->
<!-- Keep it as a single line. Examples: -->

<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** New feature in Dapr. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Bug in runtime. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **UPDATE** Runtime dependency. -->

RELEASE NOTE:

comments.body: This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has not had activity in the last 60 days. It will be closed in the next 7 days unless it is tagged (pinned, good first issue, help wanted or triaged/resolved) or other activity occurs. Thank you for your contributions.
Not sure if helpful but I've seen similar behaviour where `localhost` on MacOS is resolving to the IPv6 address which the server may not be listening on.
